Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add complete Railway deployment guide for frontend + backend

Add comprehensive guide for deploying both frontend and backend on Railway.app.

Benefits:
- Single platform for full stack
- No CORS issues (same infrastructure)
- Backend always-on (no sleep)
- Persistent filesystem for uploads
- ~$0.50/month after $5 free credit

Guide includes:
- Step-by-step setup for both services
- PostgreSQL configuration
- Environment variables setup
- Custom domains (optional)
- Persistent volumes for images
- Monitoring and troubleshooting
- Cost breakdown and optimization tips

sides (24px peek + 8px gap) so users know they can scroll. Move dot
indicators below the image. Add all 7 mountain photos to the
Discovering Hiking timeline step.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: rework gallery to card stack with slanted side cards

Center image sits on top (z-10), prev/next cards peek from behind
with 4deg rotation, 92% scale, and a dark overlay. Clicking a side
card or using arrows/dots animates the transition (500ms ease-out).
No scroll-based logic — pure state-driven with CSS transitions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add touch swipe and fix card stack overflow

Add touch swipe support (50px threshold) on the center card for
mobile navigation. Reduce side card size (80% width, 3deg rotation,
0.9 scale) and add px-4 padding to prevent cards from protruding
outside the container.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: fluid drag-to-swipe for card stack gallery

Center card follows finger in real-time during drag. Side cards
interpolate rotation/scale/overlay toward center as you drag toward
them. Releases use velocity + distance threshold to decide snap vs
bounce-back. Easing uses cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) for a
natural spring-like settle. Rubber-band effect at edges.

Phase 4 enhancements: memory-aware agents, guardrails, A/B routing, KG enrichment, observability

Agents (12 files):
- All agents now inject user profile + policy portfolio from memory into prompts
- Surrender calculator: enhanced with bonus rate tables and IRDAI surrender value rules

API:
- New KG auto-enrichment endpoint and maintenance API
- Cache invalidation support in chat and websocket

Guardrails:
- Compliance: multilingual IRDAI compliance checks (Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, Bengali, Marathi)
- Emotional: expanded crisis detection with regional language support
- Hallucination: added financial figure cross-validation

LLM:
- A/B model routing with experiment tracking and statistical analysis
- Router: added model performance tracking hooks

Orchestrator:
- Graph: added cache invalidation and memory-aware routing
- Nodes: enhanced intent classification, execution planning, guardrail checks

Services:
- KG enrichment: full auto-enrichment pipeline from IRDAI sources
- Outcome analyzer: statistical analysis of agent performance
- Outcome collector: enhanced with feedback loop integration

Memory:
- Profile layer: preference learning from conversation patterns
- Response cache: intelligent invalidation with TTL and dependency tracking

Observability:
- Cost tracker: per-agent and per-model cost breakdown
- Logger: structured context propagation
- Metrics: SLO tracking with burn rate alerts
- Tracing: distributed trace context with LangSmith integration

Tests:
- New: cache invalidation, PII guard, KG enrichment, maintenance endpoints
- Load test: rewritten with SLO validation and concurrent user simulation

Fix mobil hero-tekst bag header — øg padding-top

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

signet: implement SQLCipher store + mlock'd hot key cache

New modules:
- store.h/store.c: SQLCipher-backed persistent storage with envelope
  encryption (XSalsa20-Poly1305 via libsodium). Secret keys are double-
  encrypted: SQLCipher AES-256 at the database level, plus per-agent
  crypto_secretbox_easy with a key derived from SIGNET_DB_KEY via
  BLAKE2b KDF.
- key_store.c rewritten: wraps SignetStore with a GHashTable hot cache
  where entries are allocated via sodium_malloc (mlock'd). All agent
  keys loaded into cache at startup. Signing hot path is pointer
  dereference only. Provides provision/revoke/cache_count/is_open APIs.

Build system updated to require sqlite3 and libsodium dependencies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: instruct AI to start from verified SQL examples in specs

The AI was constructing BQ queries from scratch and picking wrong
tables (e.g. invoice_items instead of product_catalog for payment
gateway fees), even though the specs contain verified SQL examples
for exactly that use case. Now Phase 2 explicitly tells the AI to
start from verified examples and adapt them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add cloud-integrated Export Hub to dashboard (v3) (#3)

* Add cloud-integrated Export Hub to dashboard (v3)

- Full-screen slide-over drawer with 5 tabs: Templates, Share, Integrations, Schedule, History
- Templates tab: 6 pre-built export templates (Tax Report, Monthly Summary, Category Analysis,
  Bills Tracker, Food & Fun, Full Backup) with live record counts and one-click download
- Share tab: shareable link with clipboard copy, fake QR code (SVG, deterministic), email export UI
- Integrations tab: Google Sheets, Dropbox, OneDrive, Notion — simulated connect/disconnect/sync
  with connection status indicators and last-sync timestamps (persisted in localStorage)
- Schedule tab: enable toggle, frequency (daily/weekly/monthly), time picker, format selector,
  destination picker, next-run preview — all persisted in localStorage
- History tab: full log of all exports with template, format, record count, file size, timestamp
- Gradient Export Hub button replaces plain Export Data button
- New files: lib/exportHistory.ts, lib/exportTemplates.ts, components/export-hub/ExportDrawer.tsx

* Fix ESLint/TS quality issues and add project docs

- Remove unused imports (Tag, Plus, Pause, addDays/addWeeks/addMonths/startOfYear, CATEGORY_COLORS, formatCurrency, formatDate)
- Move SortButton outside ExpenseList render to fix react-hooks/static-components error
- Fix unused ternary expression in toggleSelect (replaced with if/else)
- Replace useEffect setState pattern in useExpenses with lazy useState initializer
- Add CLAUDE.md with project conventions, SOLID principles, dev workflow, and quality gates
- Add code-analysis.md with detailed comparison of all three export implementations
- Add command/ folder for saved commands

commit.message:
refactor(heartbeat): extract CronExecutor, Inspector, and CronDelivery from HeartbeatRunner

Split the monolithic HeartbeatRunner into focused components:
- CronExecutor: scheduled task execution engine
- Inspector: routine discovery and reflection gating
- CronDelivery: result delivery and notification
- RoutineManager: stuck task recovery and routine operations

Also adds InspectorSchedule to Scheduler for low-frequency observation ticks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: company_admin peut gérer les permissions de ses utilisateurs

  /users/{user}/permissions (restreint à sa propre entreprise, exclut admins)
- Frontend: bouton Shield dans la liste des utilisateurs (violet)
- Modal permissions avec 3 états: forcé actif (vert) / forcé inactif (rouge) / hérité du rôle (gris)
- Badge "override" visible quand une permission est explicitement modifiée
- Bouton "reset" pour revenir au comportement du rôle
- Rôles supplémentaires affichés: fleet_manager, rental_agent, mechanic

signet: rewrite config with file loader, env overrides, and validation

Replace stub config with full implementation:
- GKeyFile-based config file parsing ([server], [store], [nostr],
  [policy_defaults], [replay], [audit] sections)
- SIGNET_-prefixed env var overrides (always applied last)
- SIGNET_BUNKER_NSEC handling: accepts nsec1 bech32 or hex, derives
  pubkey via nostr_key_get_public()
- signet_config_validate() checks required fields
- New fields: log_level enum, health_port (int), reconnect_interval_s,
  provisioner_pubkeys, allowed_kinds, rate_limit_rpm, audit_stdout (bool)
- Removed: health_enable/health_listen (replaced by health_port),
  admin_pubkeys (renamed to provisioner_pubkeys)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: convert Patch workflow from monolithic call to play chain architecture

Replace the ~300-line Patch#call method with composable actors and a two-phase
play chain (Setup + Pipeline), consistent with FullPipeline's structure.

Key changes:
- Add InitializePatchContext actor that swaps pipeline context to patch mode
  (tracker, adw_id, logger, agent_name_prefix) so downstream actors work
  unchanged for both normal and patch pipelines
- Add MarkPatchDone actor to finalize both patch and main trackers
- Add polymorphic dispatch in Tracker.update/save via _type: :patch flag,
  eliminating the need for separate update_patch/save_patch call sites
- Add agent_name_prefix + prefixed_name helper to PipelineInputs so all
  actors generate context-aware agent names (e.g. "patch_code_reviewer")
- Make CommitChanges accept optional commit_message and issue inputs
- Make PushBranch accept push_blocking flag for non-blocking push in patches
- Add worktree auto-detection to adw_patch bin script

commit.message:
refactor: remove dead code from annotation-processor and plutus-aiken (#608)

* fix: remove registerTuplePair to fix SundaeSwapV2 Pair serialization

The shared type registry was mapping Tuple schemas to raw (unparameterized)
Pair, which caused generated converters to call Pair.toPlutusData() — a
method that doesn't exist. Removing the interception lets
SchemaTypeResolver.resolveListType() produce the correct parameterized
Pair<byte[], byte[]>, which generates proper inline ListPlutusData
serialization code.

* feat: add JpgStore Sniper contract integration test for implicit V3 stdlib

Add jpgstore/sniper-onchain blueprint (Aiken v1.1.21, Plutus V3) as an
integration test that deliberately omits @AikenStdlib to verify V3 is
the implicit default. Shared types (Address, Credential, OutputReference,
etc.) resolve via the V3 registry without explicit annotation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: remove dead code from annotation-processor and plutus-aiken

Remove unused methods, fields, and local variables identified by static
analysis across both modules: splitTypeParameters, isGenerated, clear,
JavaFileUtil.error, interfaceName field/getter, ClassDefinition.name,
Field.alternative, Type.type, bytesInternal, registerSchema,
Hash.algorithm/of factories, and an unused local in PlutusDataTypeProcessor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
